- BIG Changes in 2025 Rental Laws: Guide for California Landlords and Tenants!: Kind: captions Language: en It's that time for the 2025 Landlord/Tenant Legal Update. In this quick update, we are going to cover five big changes, including two of renters' biggest gripes, including application fees when applying for a rental, and getting your security deposit. New laws that landlords must follow. Hey there, Christian Walsh, real estate agent with WIRE Associates. We've been helping renters and landlords understand their rights before things go wrong. And don't take our word for it, we have a link to all the Civil Code so that you can read this legislation on your own. We encourage you to go directly to the source. And remember, we can't give tax or legal advice, but for the most honest and up to date real estate advice, subscribe to this channel. One of the biggest complaints renters have when it is time to apply for a rental, they can be running around town paying application fees all over the place. AB 2493 changes the way application fees are handled in the state of California. So first off, In order to collect applications and application fees, a landlord or property manager needs to have a property for rent right now or about to be available for rent. You can't just be collecting applications and holding onto them and charging people. Once that requirement is met, there now will be two ways to screen applicants. So the first way, the landlord or property manager will need to give the application plus their screening guidelines. So whatever they require for screening, meaning what minimum credit score they have, what requirements they have for income in addition to what they require to actually screen the renter. In this scenario, the landlord must choose the first applicant that meets the requirements that they give in those screening requirements. So they can't go ahead and collect a bunch of applications and then choose from all the best candidates. They have to choose the first one that meets those requirements. Any renters beyond that applicant, the landlord must refund the application fee to those renters. If the applicants don't meet those screening requirements, so for example, their credit score is lower than the minimum score that was required by the landlord and given with that application, the landlord does not have to return the application fee. It's only those who applied and meet the screening requirements and were not chosen by the landlord. The other way that a landlord can screen for a renter is that landlord who likes to collect applications and choose the best one. If a landlord is going to go this route, they can do that, but what they must do is upon picking a renter, they would have to return the application fees to all those renters they didn't choose, even if a renter didn't meet their screening requirements, in this case, the landlord must return that rental application fee. A lot of the questions I receive are related to security deposits and how much a landlord is returning. There's a new law in town, AB 2801, which is now going to require more photographic evidence. In 2025, if a landlord is going to keep security deposit, they still need to give their itemized list of deductions, but now they must include a written description of why they're deducting those funds, plus include pictures. AB 2801 will be phased in throughout 2025 and will be the law of the land going forward. So on April 1st, it will be required for a landlord, when giving the itemized deduction, to also have pictures of the rental , after move out and then after the repairs and cleaning. So two sets of photos. After July 1st of 2025, the landlord will be required to take pictures at the inception of the tenancy, which would be usually when the tenant is taking possession. So just before the tenant takes possession, the landlord will need to take pictures of the rental property. They'll still be required to have the other photos, the after move out pictures and after repair and cleaning pictures. It'll be a minimum of three sets of pictures required in order for a landlord to deduct from the security deposit for repairs or cleaning. Another big change for 2025, you make all your on time payments for a car, for your cell phone, everything in between, and those get reported to the credit bureau. But what about your rent? Well now there's a new law that allows a tenant to ask their landlord to go ahead and report their rental payments to the credit bureaus. AB 2747 outlines the positive credit reporting requirements. And again, this is optional for the renter to elect for positive credit reporting, but the landlord does have to offer it and it talks about what's required for the notice. The landlord is allowed to charge an additional monthly fee up to $10 for this fair credit reporting, whatever it actually costs to do it, and must annually ask a renter if they want to opt into fair credit reporting. Any tenancies that start after April 1st, 2025, this will be in the lease. For anything before then the landlord has to go ahead and offer the tenant the option by April 1st, 2025. Landlords who are exempt from this requirement are those who have a building that is 15 units or smaller, as long as it's not owned by a Real Estate Investment Trust or an LLC. If a landlord has more than one building, then they are subject to this, no matter how many units. SB 611 makes some changes. There's actually several things bundled into this one. The important one in here we're going to talk about is the fact that a landlord can no longer charge a fee for posting a notice to terminate tenancy. So if you get a 60 day notice to terminate tenancy, for example, a no fault termination of tenancy can't be charged for that notice and same thing with an at fault. So if you haven't paid rent and you get a three day notice to pay or quit, the landlord cannot charge you a fee for posting of that notice. There's some other goodies in here as well and I encourage you to read the Civil Code. AB 2347 makes changes to how long a renter has to answer an unlawful detainer in the event that they're being evicted. So this is typically what people think about for an eviction. They receive paperwork and it says they're going to have to go in front of a judge. Before January 1st, 2025, a renter had five court days to answer that unlawful detainer. And we've created content around that. The Tenant Power Toolkit is an important tool that you can use if you've been served the unlawful detainer paperwork as a renter. That five day deadline to answer the unlawful detainer has now been changed to 10 days to answer the unlawful detainer. And if it's been mailed, you have an additional five. So total of 15 court days to answer. So what is a court day that is anything but a Saturday, Sunday or other judicial holiday. So any weekday, any business day where court is in session, that's considered a court day. So a 10 day time period to answer actually will go several weeks by the time you include those Saturdays and Sundays. So that is a big change, giving renters more time to answer the unlawful detainer. And there's a second part related to motions that can be filed, and we're going to actually go through those when we sit down and do a live stream with our eviction attorney buddies at California Property Law Group we will dive into that deeper. If you're thinking of buying or selling, don't even think of making a move without reaching out to us first. For a deeper dive into landlord tenant regulations and changes, make sure you subscribe to our free weekly email newsletter where we cover topics like this plus a whole lot more you need to know. And speaking of what you need to know, YouTube thinks you need to watch this next video right here. Thanks for tuning in. This has been Christian Walsh, Real Estate Agent with WIRE Associates, and we can't wait to hear from you.

While Lu Dien was selling products, he suddenly saw a flood of fans pouring in, even sending roses. The viewer count shot up to ten thousand in no time. Looking at the screen, Lu Dien saw the views and hearts rising sharply and was so moved he nearly cried. “The audience wants to see me eat? I can sing and dance too. What’s going on?” A Thach said, “Today’s livestream is over.” Lu Dien was still in a daze. “Wait, did the company buy me fans?” A Thach flew into a rage. “In your dreams. Where would we get that kind of money? Last time the company spent a huge sum buying you a hundred thousand followers, and it still didn’t create any buzz.” Lu Dien said, “Could it be everyone has finally discovered my charm? But I was clearly working hard before, and still...” Then their investor daddy, Mr. Ngo, appeared cheerfully. “Lu Dien, you’ve worked hard. This round of sales has completely exceeded expectations. I already signed a deal with your boss for salted eggs and pickled cucumbers. You won’t need any extra styling from now on. I think you’re really suited for selling things. You’re truly our star—the star of agricultural products.” Lu Dien froze. “What the hell?” Mr. Ngo continued, “But you’re even cuter up close than you are on screen. Cute boy, I think very highly of you.” “Thank you, Mr. Ngo. I’ll work hard.” “Our little Dien really is lovable.” Even though Lu Dien was being praised, he was deeply unhappy inside. The company insists on making me play some cute little boy. Every day I have to smile and act spoiled—I'm one step away from wearing a skirt. And what do you mean, star of agricultural products? Soft little brother, bunny boy, fluffy white bunny, little beauty, little princess. ‘Mommy wants a hug.’ ‘The most beautiful guy in women’s clothing.’ Why didn’t you stop it?” A Thach smacked him on the head. “You really are too immature.” “The one drawing you is the famous artist Hua San. She has nearly ten million fans and is the top figure in the doujin world. She can revive fandoms that seemed totally dead. She’s a goddess recognized by the entire circle and famous everywhere. The law of ancestral property says that if a property is three generations old. Now people often think that I am going to clear the concept of these two which is four generations old. See if the property has been purchased by your three ancestors earlier or if it is four generations old. Both things are the same. For example, if your father's father's father, that is, your great grandfather, is called great grandfather. When the great grandfather died, that is, when the great grandfather died, that property came to your grandfather. Your grandfather died and it came to your father, your father died and now it has come to you, in this way that property has passed after four generations and the most important thing is that no one has divided this property between them, neither your grandfather nor your father nor your great grandfather, so as soon as this property crosses four generations without any division, it will turn into ancestral property. Now a special rule of ancestral property is that whichever heir is born in that family, note that it is not necessary that he should be 18 years old at the time of birth, as soon as he is born, he automatically gets a share in that ancestral property and no one can sell that share, gift it or bequeath it and even if someone wants to sell it, then first permission has to be taken from the court that this person is a co-parcener here. That of the minor who was entitled to a share in the ancestral property. Can be sold by taking permission from the court or due to legal necessity and benefit of the estate. This is the law of ancestral property. And on the other hand, if we talk about self- acquired property, it is that which a person has purchased himself. He did not get the ancestral lands of his great grandfather. Bought it from my own earnings. Or someone else bequeathed it, gifted it , or sold it. So in all these three conditions, the property you are getting will be considered your self- acquired property. There is only one rule of self-acquired property that the person who has acquired it has sole rights over it and during his lifetime neither his children nor his wife nor any third person will be able to claim any rights over that property. When he dies, then the heirs of class one, his son and daughter, if the son and daughter die, then his children, mother, his wife, they can have rights over it, when he died and did not leave a will, this is the law of self-acquired property. Now friends, it is often seen that cases are going on in the court, people do not know which is their ancestral property, which is their proprietary, whether it becomes their share or not and due to ignorance, due to lack of complete knowledge of the law, due to lack of any relevant judgment, a case is filed directly. They remain stuck in the court for years and ultimately lose the case. Now I will discuss with you the latest judgment of the Supreme Court. Friends, please pay attention by looking at the display once. The case title is Angadi Chandranna, Appellant herein, versus Shankar Others, Respondent herein. The most important thing is the civil appeal number 5401 of 2025 and the date of judgment is 22nd April 2025. Now, what happened in the present case was that there was an ancestral property and in the year 1986, that ancestral property was partitioned and after the partition, the ancestral property becomes self-acquired property, so when one brother got his share after the partition, it became his own. He sold that portion to another brother. It's simple brother, the division has happened. Whatever share everyone was getting, they earned it themselves. He can sell it to anyone, the law will not say anything about it. So under the same law, he sold that share to his other brother because the partition had already taken place. After some time a claim was filed in the court. It was said in the court that this is ancestral property. How can one sell ancestral property to another person ? Everyone has a right to that. The court here accepted that this is absolutely correct. It was ancestral property and could not be sold here and the trial court gave the verdict in favour of the plaintiff. An appeal is made later. It is seen in the appeal that the partition deed was made in the year 1986. And after the ancestral property is divided under partition, whoever gets the share becomes his own. Sarjeet gets angry. So on that basis this property was sold. And that person has every right to sell it as he wishes. So that's why he sold it and whoever got that property, it became his own. Now this matter has been further appealed and has gone to the Supreme Court. Two questions arose before the Supreme Court. First, whether ancestral property automatically becomes acquired by people after partition. Second, which property will be called ancestral? The Supreme Court has clearly stated here that if the ancestral property has been divided then that ancestral property will no longer remain your joint family property. Whatever share a person gets after the division is his own earning. If he wants, he can sell it, keep it or gift it, it is his own wish. No other person can object to this. The second thing that was told was that three ancestral properties would be considered ancestral. That means, when it comes from great grandfather to grandfather, from grandfather to your father and from father to you without any division, it will become your ancestral property. The Supreme Court clarified both these important things in its judgment. Now here you have to pay attention to one more thing that in what ways can the ancestral property be divided so that it becomes self-acquired. You can do the partition deed sitting at home. Family settlement document can be made and registered. Verbal division can be done. Vineeta Sharma vs Rakesh Sharma Supreme Court judgment came on 11 August 2020. It has been clearly stated that oral division will also be considered absolutely valid. The fourth method is to make a memorandum after verbal division. It is not even necessary to get it registered. The work will be done at low cost. Another way is to file a partition suit in the court and get a degree. By adopting these five methods, you can divide your ancestral property and by dividing it, you can make it your own property. Parents tend to distribute property by saying that the higher level siblings has everything already. So let's give more property to the lower level one as he needs it more.
This is wrong.
You never know what kind of situations may arrive for the higher level one in future. What if the former passes away or looses his job.

There is a lot of confusion in your minds regarding ancestral property and self-acquired property. Now what does ancestral property mean? The Supreme Court of India has said in this judgment that if there is any ancestral property and if the share of the ancestral property is divided, then after the division, those people who have got the share after the division of the ancestral property, then brother, the property that has come to all of you, will it be considered as yours now, it will be considered as self-acquired property, the right on the ancestral property has ended, all the property has now come to you after being distributed, now it is self-acquired property, now if you want to sell that property, sell it to some other person. - पैतृक संपत्ति में सिर्फ अपना हिस्सा अपने नाम कैसे कराएं || Ancestral Property || Partition Suit Law: Kind: captions Language: en Hi friends, how to claim your share in a joint family property? In a family, property is shared. Friends, if someone wants their share, how can they claim it? An uncle doesn't want a partition, a brother does n't want a partition, or a father doesn't want the joint family property divided. What should be done in such a situation? Friends, the law and code become the only recourse. Friends, filing a suit for partition in court is the only way to divide the property. By claiming partition, you can claim only your share, meaning the others will remain joint owners. The court will allocate your share separately. This is called separation of property. On the other hand, partition is when the court allocates everyone's shares. If someone wants their share or wants the entire property (joint property) divided, then in such a situation, they have to file a suit for partition in court. Friends, there are some conditions. The first thing that the person has to do is send a legal notice, that is, they have to properly serve notice to the other stakeholders that they are not part of the property. I am a shareholder, you are also a joint shareholder in it and if we have to request that this property be divided among us, then let me tell you a very important thing that you are the owner of the joint property, you have a share in it, everyone is a shareholder in it, it is not a new property, the distribution of dating rights is the right that you already have, there is a division of those rights, that is in the property rights, tomorrow your share will be separated or the share of other people will also be separated, the property will remain the same, therefore friends, this is also a distribution of existing rights, because of this if you write this in your petition, in your suit, that you also have a right on this property, this property is also in your possession, you will not have to pay any court fee, friends, I am telling you a very important thing, people say that in filing a partition suit, you will have to pay court fees equal to the value of the property or its part, it is not so, friends, if you show that any property is also in your possession, then you will have to pay only ₹20, in many states 50 and in some states 100 rupees. This is the court fee, before that the Disciplinary Judge has to take a decision which is called preliminary degree. A degree sheet is prepared along with the judgment order in which it is clearly written whether you have rights or not or which property you get, it is called decree. It is the text of the judgment, it is the text of the order, so the preliminary decree has to be passed by the court, which means that in the first stage, in the suit for partition, the court decides whether you have an affair, whether you are a shareholder in the joint property of your family, it is decided first in this degree, friends, if you are a shareholder, then can you be given a separate share, can you be given your share, its decision is given in its preliminary free, after that, the important stage is how to divide the property and which share will go to whom, if it is agricultural land, friends, then the court instructs the collector to make a division plan for its division and consult them, mostly in agricultural land, since the party reaches the collector's office, things are explained there, understanding is also made between the parties. It is decided who can take which share. In this way, there are not many disputes. If no party has any objection, then a report comes from the collector's office that it has been divided in this manner and ultimately the property is divided. But if it is an ancestral house, friends, a family house or a big building, a residential house, there are a lot of problems in their division. For that, a commissioner is appointed. That commissioner can be anyone, an architect, a lawyer. How can a commissioner be appointed? Or sometimes the collector of the district is given the authority to have more commissioners visit there and prepare a report on how this constructed property will be divided. After that report, if any party has any objection, then they have to present their views in the court and then the court announces a final decree. The court will give its respect. Friends, on the basis of the final degree, this division of the property will be completed. This is the method, friends, from suit for partition to preliminary sale and then final degree and judgment. If even then a party maintains its possession over any land or any part of it, does not leave it and does not give it to the other party, which is called handover, then the other party or the party which is agreed, feels that it has not received its share even after passing the degree and after the court order, then it can file an execution. An execution petition has to be filed in the court. The court sends the police force, the officers there, the video, the Shiva, are sent to them and these things are divided by the police force. Friends, 12 years is the limit period, that is, you have to file this for 12 years for the partition, but when will the counting of this 12 years start, let me tell you, if someone has kept someone's share, has suppressed it and in this way, these things have become common in the society, people know that he has taken possession of that part, if this belief is spread socially, then from the time when the belief has been prevalent in the society, within 12 years from that time, the agreed party whose share has been taken over by someone It is suppressed and is not being given to him. That party will file a partition suit in the court in 12 years, friends, this is its counting and at the same time, if any degree is passed in your favor and finally even then you do not get your affair, now the other party is occupying it, even then you have 12 years friends to execute your decree, file execution, to take your share in your possession, you will have 12 years to take it in your fashion, this is some legal CG friends and a case of 20-30 piece suit for partition can be filed for this.

👉 एक व्यक्ति ने वसीयत रजिस्टर्ड करवाई और अपनी सारी कृषि भूमि अपने पूर्वजों के द्वारा बनवाए गए मंदिर के नाम कर दी 
कृषि भूमि भी पूर्वजों से ही आई थी ऐसा उन्होंने अपनी वसीयत में लिखा 
वसीयत कर्ता ने कहा कि अब मेरे उतराधिकारी इस मंदिर और कृषि भूमि पर सिर्फ प्रबंधन करें और प्रयोग करें मंदिर और सम्पत्ति कृषि भूमि को सुरक्षित रखें और पीढ़ी दर पीढ़ी ऐसा ही चलने दे लेकिन किसी को भी इस सम्पत्ति कृषि भूमि को बेचने का अधिकार नहीं होगा और अगर कोई बेचे तो मंदिर के अनुसार निराधार होगा उसे इस सम्पत्ति का कर्ता धरता होने का अधिकार न रहेगा भाग्यवश कभी ऐसा समय आए तो अंतिम उतराधिकारी बाहर से किसी योग्य लड़के को गोद लेकर इस मंदिर का पुजारी, कृषि भूमि प्रबंधक और कर्ता धरता हो जाएगा 
समय बीता और वंशज बढ़े और उनकी आवशताएं और लालच भी 
वंशजों ने एक सिविल कोर्ट में मुकदमा दर्ज कराया और कोर्ट को बताया कि हम पुजारी हैं और मंदिर से आय नहीं हो रही है और पुजारी की संख्या बढ़ गई है और ये हमारे पूर्वज ने एक प्राईवेट ट्रस्ट बनाया था जिसे हम आपसी सहमति से भंग करना चाहते हैं
और वंशजों ने बताया कि मंदिर समाप्त हो गया है वहां कोई पूजा, अनुष्ठान नहीं हो रहा है वहां कोई मूर्ति आदि नहीं है 
और वंशजों ने आपस में वादी प्रतिवादी बनकर और अपने दो वकील जो आपस में सीनियर जूनियर थे उनके द्वारा आपसी सहमति को बताया और एक डिक्री प्राप्त कर ली 
और ये वादी प्रतिवादी जो वसीयत कर्ता के वंशज थे उस सारी कृषि भूमि को अपने नाम कुर्रे करवा दिया और बेचने लगे 
अब इन वंशजों ने न्यायालय को यह भी बताया कि हमारे अलावा कोई और वंशज नहीं है 
जबकि वसीयत कर्ता का एक वंशज जो इस समय सेवानिवृत्त सैनिक है उसको इन्होंने एक वसीयत रजिस्टर्ड के माध्यम से बेदखल कर दिया 
अब वो व्यक्ति उस डिक्री पारित को कैंसल कराने के लिए मुकदमा दर्ज कराया है सम्पत्ति कृषि भूमि बहुत ज्यादा थी जो उन वंशजों ने बेची और बेचकर अन्य सम्पत्ति कृषि भूमि, इमारतें आदि बनवाया 

अब ये लोग यह साबित नहीं कर पा रहे हैं कि पूर्वज वसीयत कर्ता ने जो रजिस्टर्ड करवाई थी उन्होंने किसी प्राइवेट ट्रस्ट की स्थापना की थी क्योंकि उनके वसीयत रजिस्टर्ड के अनुसार तो समस्त सम्पत्ति कृषि भूमि मंदिर की हुई 
शर्मा जी आपसे इतना ही अनुरोध है कि आप कोई मार्गदर्शन करें 
अपने विचारों के माध्यम से कि इन वंशजों और इनके वकीलों का क्या करें 
मंदिर विराजमान हैं लेकिन सिर्फ जमीन पर कागजों में कोर्ट का निर्णय में लिखा गया है कि मंदिर समाप्त हो गया है। 
क्या यह फर्जीवाड़ा है या नहीं 
आप के उत्तर की प्रतीक्षा में आपका शुभचिंतक

  19. @shivkumarsingh7493: सर जी नमस्कार सर जी हमारे दादा जी पहले खत्म हो गए थे उनके नाम को जमीन में पड़ गया था उसके बाद हमारे छोटे दादा खत्म हुए उनके नाम को जमीन में पड़ गया है वह भी मौजूद है उसके बाद सबसे लास्ट में हमारे बड़े दादा खत्म हुए उनके नाम ज्यादा संपत्ति है हम लोग बराबर के हकदार हैं और हम सब एक ही परिवार के हैं मगर हमारे पर दादा के तीन बेटे थे जिम सबसे बड़े थे,1 पार सिंह 2 शिवराज सिंह, 3 हाकिम सिंह तो कुछ जमीन में हमारे दादा का और छोटे दादा का नाम पढ़ने से रह गया है उसमें हम लोग बराबर के हकदार थे और हम लोगों को गला मिल रहा था हमने शामिल में बोरिंग जो करवाया है उसमें हमने पैसे भी दिए अब उन लोगों की नियत खराब हो गई हमारे परिवार वालों की उन्होंने हमें करीब 18 साल से गल नहीं दिया है अब मैं इसकी कार्रवाई के लिए 1076 पर शिकायत की है यह लोग धोखाधड़ी करके गलत रिपोर्ट साबित कर देते हैं सबूत हम अपने पास निकाल कर रख लेते हैं इन्होंने कुछ जमीन खतौनी से रिकॉर्ड रूम से गायब करवा दी इसके सबूत हमारे पास है हमें आप इसका जवाब जरूर दें हमें आपका इंतजार है क्या हम इन पर कौन सा मुकदमा डालें जिसमें हम बराबर के हकदार बने जिसमें नाम छूट गया है आप की महान कृपा होगी इसका जवाब अवश्य दें
